summary: Host-targeted AES-decrypted payload executed via new Function() on require()
details: Package self-describes as a TypeScript linter but on require() of the main entry (index.js) silently loads lib/perf.js, which reads /etc/machine-id, uses it as an AES-128-CBC key with a hardcoded IV to decrypt an 896-hex-char ciphertext blob, and executes the resulting JavaScript via `new Function(r)()`. The decryption key is the victim host's machine-id, so only the targeted machine can recover and run the payload; on every other host the nested try/catch swallows the failure and the package appears inert. There is no legitimate reason for a linter to perform host-keyed AES decryption of opaque bytes followed by dynamic code evaluation at module-load time. Loading this package on the targeted Linux host silently runs attacker-controlled code with the installer's privileges — a targeted backdoor delivered through the npm supply chain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
